did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper discusses AI-enabled mHealth applications in dermatology, specifically citing a case study of skin cancer detection via smartphone imaging. It references a convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on a large dataset of skin lesion images that achieved dermatologist-level performance (87% agreement) in classifying skin lesions as benign or malignant.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The abstract explicitly describes the work as a 'comprehensive survey' that 'examines AI-enabled mHealth across four domains.' The introduction states the paper provides 'a comprehensive survey of AI in mHealth' and 'consolidating technical, clinical, and practical insights into a unified perspective.' It references prior surveys and reviews in the Related Work section and aims to provide an 'updated and thorough reference that builds upon and extends the existing related work,' which are characteristics of a review or survey paper rather than an original experimental study.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
